---
title: Setup
---

Please complete these steps **before** the tutorial begins.
If you run into trouble, find us before the session starts or post in the workshop chat.

:::{.callout-important}
## Python Version
Please make sure you are using a version of Python **BELOW** 3.15

Any of Python 3.11.x, 3.12.x, 3.13.x, 3.14.x will be fine.

Check the GitHub Actions of the install tests on different python versions here: <https://github.com/chendaniely/pycon-2026-shiny/actions/workflows/test-install.yml>
:::

## Python Environment

Pick **one** of the following methods — they all produce the same result.

Here are the packages you'll be installing:

```{.text filename="requirements.txt"}
{{< include ../requirements.txt >}}
```


::: {.panel-tabset}

### python + venv

```bash
python -m venv .venv

# macOS / Linux
source .venv/bin/activate

# Windows
.venv\Scripts\activate

pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### conda

```bash
conda create -n pycon2026 python=3.12
conda activate pycon2026
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### uv

```bash
uv venv .venv

# macOS / Linux
source .venv/bin/activate

# Windows
.venv\Scripts\activate

uv pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### pixi

```bash
pixi init .
pixi add python==3.12 pip
pixi run python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
pixi shell
```

:::

## IDE

We recommend [Positron](https://positron.posit.co/), this is what the workshop will be using.
[VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) works just as well.

Whichever you choose, install the
[Shiny extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=Posit.shiny)
(it works in both).

::: {.callout-note}
We will **not** be using Jupyter Notebooks during this workshop.
:::

## Run in the Cloud

No local install needed — see the [Cloud](cloud.qmd) page for GitHub Codespaces, MyBinder, and Posit Cloud instructions.

:::{.callout-warning}
It's best to do a local installation of everything you need.
The cloud experience will be slow, and some things may not work.

This is really provided as a backup.
:::


## AI / Chat Model

### GitHub Models (free, recommended)

You'll need a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT).
It doesn't require any special scopes.

1. [Create a classic PAT](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#creating-a-personal-access-token-classic)
   following GitHub's instructions
2. Set the token as an environment variable:

```bash
export GITHUB_TOKEN=your_token_here   # macOS / Linux
$env:GITHUB_TOKEN="your_token_here"   # Windows PowerShell
```

### Local LLM via Ollama (optional, no account needed)

1. Download and install [Ollama](https://ollama.com/)
2. Pull a model that fits your machine:

| Model | Download Size | Install Command |
|---|---|---|
| qwen3:0.6b | 523 MB | `ollama run qwen3:0.6b` |
| phi4-mini-reasoning | 3.2 GB | `ollama run phi4-mini-reasoning` |
| qwen3 | 5.2 GB | `ollama run qwen3` |
| devstral | 14 GB | `ollama run devstral` |

### Paid API (optional)

::: {.callout-note}
Consumer subscriptions (Claude.ai, ChatGPT Plus, etc.) and API access are billed separately.
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Anthropic Claude

1. Sign up at <https://console.anthropic.com>
2. Add credits
3. Create an API key at <https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys>

```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

#### OpenAI

1. Sign up at <https://openai.com/>
2. Create an API key at <https://platform.openai.com/api-keys>

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

#### Google Gemini

1. Sign in at <https://aistudio.google.com>
2. Click **Create API key**

```bash
export GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

:::

## Verify Your Installation

1. Clone or download this repository: <https://github.com/chendaniely/pycon-2026-shiny>
2. Activate your Python environment

**Step 1 — check that all packages import correctly:**

```bash
python test-install.py
```

You should see a `✓` next to every package and a final `All good!` message.

**Step 2 — check that Shiny is working:**

```bash
shiny run test-install.py
```

You should see:

```
INFO:     Started server process [...]
INFO:     Waiting for application startup.
INFO:     Application startup complete.
INFO:     Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
```

Open <http://127.0.0.1:8000> in your browser and confirm the page loads.
